# CircleCI CLI

Remove an environment variable from a CircleCI context.

This action is irreversible. Jobs that depend on this variable will
fail until a new value is set.

Pass a UUID to identify the context by ID, or a name to look up by name.
When looking up by name, pass --org or run from a git repository so the
organization can be inferred from the remote.

In a terminal, you will be prompted to confirm before deleting.
Use --force (-f) to skip the prompt for scripting.

## Usage

`circleci context secret delete <context-id|context-name> [flags]`

## Flags

| Flag            | Description                                                       |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `-f, --force`   | skip confirmation prompt                                          |
| `--name string` | Name of the environment variable to delete                        |
| `--org string`  | Organization slug (e.g. gh/myorg); used when resolving name to ID |

## Inherited Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                  |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `-c, --config string` | path to config file (default: ~/.config/circleci/config.yml) |
| `--debug`             | enable debug logging                                         |
| `-q, --quiet`         | suppress informational output; data on stdout is unaffected  |

## Examples

- Delete a variable (with confirmation): 
  `circleci context secret delete ctx-uuid-here --name MY_SECRET`
- Delete without confirmation: 
  `circleci context secret delete ctx-uuid-here --name MY_SECRET --force`

## Learn More

- Use `circleci <command> <subcommand> --help` for more information about a command.
- Read the manual at <https://circleci.com/docs/local-cli>
- Support at <https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/issues>
